Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze a provided event log of customer support ticket resolutions I need to identify cases with long resolution times find root causes escalations wait times etc and propose insights recommendations

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data I need to go through each Case ID and calculate the total duration from Receive Ticket to Close Ticket

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 101 Start 2024 03 01 08 00 End 2024 03 01 10 15 Duration 2 hours 15 minutes Path Receive Triage Assign Investigate Resolve Close Escalations None Wait times Receive Triage 10 mins Triage Assign 10 mins Assign Investigate 40 mins Investigate Resolve 1 hour Resolve Close 15 mins Observation Very fast standard flow

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 102 Start 2024 03 01 08 05 End 2024 03 02 09 15 Duration 25 hours 10 minutes Path Receive Triage Assign Escalate Investigate Resolve Close Escalations Yes Level 1 to Level 2 Wait times Receive Triage 25 mins Triage Assign 30 mins Assign Escalate 2 point 5 hours Escalate Investigate 2 point 5 hours Same day Investigate Resolve Overnight 19 hours Resolve Close 15 mins Observation Long duration Major delay is between Investigate Issue Level 2 and Resolve Ticket

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 103 Start 2024 03 01 08 10 End 2024 03 01 09 30 Duration 1 hour 20 minutes Path Receive Triage Assign Investigate Resolve Close Escalations None Wait times All very short 5 to 15 mins Observation Fastest case Standard flow

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 104 Start 2024 03 01 08 20 End 2024 03 02 08 30 Duration 24 hours 10 minutes Path Receive Triage Assign Investigate Resolve Close Escalations None Wait times Receive Triage 40 mins Triage Assign 30 mins Assign Investigate 3 point 5 hours Investigate Resolve Overnight 19 hours Resolve Close 30 mins Observation Long duration No escalation but a massive gap between Investigate Issue and Resolve Ticket overnight

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 105 Start 2024 03 01 08 25 End 2024 03 03 09 30 Duration 2 days 1 hour Path Receive Triage Assign Investigate Escalate Investigate Resolve Close Escalations Yes Level 1 to Level 2 Wait times Receive Triage 10 mins Triage Assign 25 mins Assign Investigate 10 mins Investigate Escalate 1 hour Gap 1 Escalate to Level 2 Investigate 24 hours Gap 2 Level 2 Investigate to Resolve 19 hours Observation Longest duration Escalation involved Significant gaps between Level 2 activities

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Conclusion Cases 102 104 and 105 are outliers compared to 101 and 103

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: All long cases have a gap crossing from one calendar day to the next specifically afternoon to next morning

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests a End of Day stoppage or a lack of shift handover protocols

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The data shows delays occurring over the weekend Tickets received Friday afternoon are sitting idle until Monday morning

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Calendar Check March 1 2024 is indeed a Friday March 2 is Saturday March 3 is Sunday

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Correction March 3 2024 is a Sunday So Case 105 was resolved on Sunday morning This implies the support team is working weekends or the ticket stayed open over the weekend and was resolved immediately when the team came back

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Re evaluating the Root Cause It is not just Weekend It is specifically the gap between Investigation and Resolution when the ticket moves past a certain time in the day such as after 13 00 or 14 00

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 104 Fri 13 00 Investigate to Sat 08 00 Resolve 19 hours Case 102 Fri 14 00 Investigate to Sat 09 00 Resolve 19 hours Pattern Any ticket where Investigate Issue happens after about 13 00 or 14 00 seems to wait until the next morning to be resolved

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This suggests tickets investigated late in the day are not resolved until the next morning regardless of whether it is a weekend or weekday This is a End of Shift policy where tickets are queued for the next shift rather than closed immediately

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 105 took longer because of the Escalation gap Escalate Fri 10 00 Investigate Level 2 Sat 14 00 That is a 28 hour wait for Level 2 to even start investigating This is a huge bottleneck specific to Escalation handover

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Refined Root Cause 1 Shift or Day Boundary Late day investigations wait until next morning to resolve 2 Escalation Handover Escalated tickets wait significantly longer for Level 2 to pick them up 3 Level 2 Availability Level 2 seems to have slower intake or specific hours compared to Level 1

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendation Implement weekend coverage SLA definitions that account for non working hours or automated triage to reduce Investigate start time
